title: Meeting Prep Agent (GTM)
problem: Executives and AEs walk into meetings cold because assembling account notes and company news into a prep email is a manual, time-consuming task.
solution: Coordinator runs simultaneous web, LinkedIn, and SharePoint searches about attendees and the company, then drafts a prep email with agenda, talking points, and suggested questions.
whoItsFor: Demand Gen, Partner Marketing Teams, AEs
workflowSteps: Intake: attendee list, company name, meeting context
Parallel searches: (1) web search for company news, (2) LinkedIn search for attendee profiles, (3) SharePoint search for past account interactions
Drafts prep email: agenda, talking points, suggested questions, account context
Sends to exec/AE ahead of meeting
keyInsights: Parallel search fan-out with a merge coordinator is the pattern. Token budget matters — three parallel searches can return large results; the coordinator must summarize each source to a token-bounded summary before merging, not pass raw results to the final LLM.

relatedExams.why: Agents and Workflows — parallel subagent fan-out
Claude Models, Prompting & Context Engineering — context compression

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([Attendees + Meeting Context]) --> B[Coordinator]
  B --> C1[Web Search: Company News]
  B --> C2[LinkedIn: Attendee Profiles]
  B --> C3[SharePoint: Account History]
  C1 & C2 & C3 --> D[Token-Bounded Merge]
  D --> E[Draft Prep Email: Agenda + Talking Points]
  E --> F([Exec or AE Inbox])
architectureNotes: The agent ingests the meeting invite (attendee list, company names) and pulls CRM data, recent news, and LinkedIn profiles. A synthesis model produces a briefing document: attendee bios, company context, open opportunities, recommended talking points, and risk flags. The briefing is delivered to the sales rep 30 minutes before the meeting via email and CRM note.

failureModes.mode: LinkedIn profile data not found for attendee
failureModes.mitigation: Graceful degradation: brief includes available data (CRM history, company news) and flags missing profile with manual lookup suggestion.

failureModes.mode: News API returns irrelevant or negative articles about the company
failureModes.mitigation: Relevance filter (company name exact match + recency weight); sentiment classifier flags negative news for explicit inclusion with recommended framing.

failureModes.mode: Briefing delivered too late (< 5 min before meeting)
failureModes.mitigation: Scheduled trigger fires 40 minutes before meeting start; SLA alert if briefing not delivered 30 minutes before; retry with degraded content if full run fails.

scalingConsiderations: Sales team scale: enterprise sales teams of 50–200 reps each doing 3–5 meetings/day = 150–1000 briefings/day; async queue handles burst; priority for meetings starting within 2 hours.
Data freshness: LinkedIn data cached for 24h; company news fetched fresh for each meeting; CRM data always fetched live.
Cost profile: ~$0.15–0.30/briefing at claude-3-5-haiku for summarization + claude-3-5-sonnet for synthesis; < $50/day for 200 meetings.

integrations.note: Meeting invite triggers briefing generation workflow
Account history, open opportunities, previous interactions
Recent company news, press releases, earnings
Delivers formatted briefing 30 min before meeting
